evidence: The paper uses Gini-based impurity importance for RF and XGB, reporting that "maternal age and z-scores of birth weight were the most important predictors using ML (RF and XGB)" versus "resuscitation and white matter disease" for LR/EN. It interprets this ranking difference as evidence that ML "detects non-linear trends," but provides no permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance corroboration of these rankings. The main conclusions about model performance (AUC 0.79 for RF vs 0.73 for LR) are based on robust held-out test set validation, but the secondary claims about which variables drive predictions rely uncorroborated on importance magnitude/ranking.
